items.title: Stock Market Today: Dow, S&P Live Updates for April 17 - Bloomberg
items.author: www.bloomberg.com
items.snippet: As of April 17, 2026, global markets are navigating significant geopolitical and macroeconomic headwinds. A potential Iran ceasefire has provided some relief, with oil prices slipping — West Texas Intermediate crude fell roughly 1.6% to around $93/barrel on Trump's optimism about a permanent peace deal. However, equities remain under pressure as policymakers, including the IMF and World Bank, warn that investors are underestimating the economic toll of ongoing conflict. Global rate paths have sh

items.title: Stock market today: Dow rises, S&P 500 and Nasdaq notch fresh records as war resolution hopes grow
items.author: finance.yahoo.com
items.snippet: U.S. equity markets surged to record highs this week, with the S&P 500 closing above 7,000 for the first time in history on April 15 (settling at 7,041.28) and the Nasdaq posting its 12th consecutive positive session — its longest winning streak since 2009. The primary catalyst has been growing optimism around a resolution to the Iran conflict, with the U.S. and Iran in indirect talks to extend their two-week ceasefire beyond April 22, and Israel-Lebanon also agreeing to a 10-day ceasefire. Oil

items.title: Global Economic Outlook: Analyzing the IMF's April 2026 Report — Investing.com
items.author: www.investing.com
items.snippet: Global markets are navigating a complex environment on April 17, 2026, shaped by ongoing U.S.-Iran geopolitical tensions and their economic aftershocks. The IMF's April 2026 World Economic Outlook projects global growth slowing to 3.1% in 2026 and 3.2% in 2027, with the Iran conflict cited as a key headwind that has disrupted the prior year's economic healing trajectory. Oil prices remain elevated near $100/barrel, rekindling inflation concerns and pushing bond yields higher — 10-year U.S. Treas

items.author: www.imf.org
items.snippet: Global markets remain under pressure in April 2026 as the cumulative effects of U.S. tariffs weigh on economic sentiment. The IMF's April 2026 World Economic Outlook projects global growth at 2.6–3.1% for 2026, well below pre-pandemic averages, citing trade fragmentation, tighter financial conditions, and geopolitical disruption as headwinds. items.title: Prediction Markets Are Turning Into a Bot Playground
items.author: www.financemagnates.com
items.snippet: Prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket have rapidly evolved into a bot-dominated ecosystem in 2026. A review of Polymarket's public leaderboard found that 14 of the 20 most profitable wallets are bots, operating 24/7 across hundreds of markets simultaneously. The most common automated strategies include cross-platform arbitrage (exploiting price discrepancies between Kalshi and Polymarket for the same event), intra-platform YES/NO mispricing, and logical mismatch detection between correla

items.title: Generating Alpha: A Hybrid AI-Driven Trading System (arxiv 2601.19504)
items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: Recent research (2025–2026) on machine learning trading strategies is dominated by three converging themes. First, hybrid AI systems that combine classical technical indicators (EMA, MACD, RSI, Bollinger Bands) with modern ML models (XGBoost, FinBERT sentiment analysis) are gaining traction — a 2026 ComSIA paper reported a 135% return over 24 months using such a regime-adaptive system. Second, deep reinforcement learning (DRL) — particularly ensemble strategies and DDPG with GRU networks — consi

items.title: Language Model Guided Reinforcement Learning in Quantitative Trading
items.author: arxiv.org
items.snippet: Recent research on reinforcement learning (RL) trading agents has accelerated significantly through 2025-2026. Key developments include ensemble strategies combining PPO, A2C, and DDPG actor-critic algorithms for automated stock trading, as well as LLM-guided RL systems where language model 'Strategist' and 'Analyst' agents generate monthly market strategies that augment base DDQN agents. A March 2025 paper explores xLSTM networks integrated into both actor and critic components with PPO optimiz

items.title: GitHub - octavi42/prediction-market-maker: Market-making strategy #2 in Paradigm's Prediction Market Challenge
items.author: github.com
items.snippet: GitHub hosts a rich ecosystem of market making strategy implementations spanning classical quantitative finance models to modern DeFi applications. The most academically grounded implementations focus on the Avellaneda-Stoikov model — a stochastic control framework for optimal bid/ask spread placement based on inventory risk and volatility. The mmssss/hft-market-making repo provides a full HFT backtesting simulator with Stoikov strategy applied to Binance Perpetual Futures data. For reinforcemen
